data.narrative:
Pasqal is a pre-IPO quantum computing company founded by a team including Alain Aspect. The target operates in a sector with limited public comparables—three traded peers (IONQ, RGTI, QBTS)—and a small private peer set of six companies, including Quantinuum, IonQ, D-Wave Quantum, Rigetti Computing, and Infleqtion. Pasqal has raised €340M+ to date.

Peer-median valuation analysis is not applicable in this case. The quantum computing sector lacks sufficient same-stage or adjacent-stage comparables meeting minimum thresholds (need ≥3 datapoints; found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier peers). While five same-sector companies exist, none satisfy the narrow filters required for reliable peer triangulation. An all-sector median approach would introduce material distortion given the heterogeneity in quantum technology approaches, business models, and commercialization timelines across the identified peer set.

Without sufficient homogeneous comparables, traditional multiples-based valuation cannot be defensibly applied. A credible range estimate would require supplementary methods—venture-capital method build-ups, DCF analysis using disclosed technology milestones, or explicit revenue/pathway assumptions—none of which are present in the available data. The pre-IPO stage and capital-intensive R&D profile further limit reliance on early-stage venture multiples. Valuation support is therefore constrained pending additional financial disclosures or market transactions among closer-stage peers.
